---
title: "Thinking with Agents"
subtitle: "AI tools for teaching and research"
page-layout: full
body-classes: landing-page
title-block-style: none
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<section class="landing-hero-split">
  <div class="hero-left">
    <h1>Thinking with<br>Agents</h1>
    <p class="hero-subtitle">AI tools for teaching and research</p>
    <p class="hero-meta">Erkmen G. Aslim &amp; Emily Beam</p>
  </div>
  <div class="hero-right">
    <h2 class="schedule-heading">Upcoming Events</h2>
    <div class="schedule-item">
      <div class="schedule-date">Jun 15</div>
      <div class="schedule-title">AI for Research (Webinar)</div>
      <div class="schedule-detail">10:00 AM–12:00 PM ET · Online</div>
      <a href="webinars.html" class="slides-btn">Register →</a>
    </div>
    <div class="schedule-item">
      <div class="schedule-date">Jun 17</div>
      <div class="schedule-title">AI for Teaching (Webinar)</div>
      <div class="schedule-detail">10:00 AM–12:00 PM ET · Online</div>
      <a href="webinars.html" class="slides-btn">Register →</a>
    </div>
    <div class="schedule-item">
      <div class="schedule-date">Jul–Aug</div>
      <div class="schedule-title">RA Intensive Bootcamp</div>
      <div class="schedule-detail">Cohort-based · Online · Sliding scale pricing</div>
      <a href="bootcamp.html" class="slides-btn">Learn more →</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>
```

::: {.landing-content}

[What We Do]{.landing-section-title}

We run hands-on workshops and bootcamps that teach economists and research professionals how to use AI tools effectively — for research workflows, teaching, and field coordination.

Our approach is **discipline-specific** (economics and development research, not generic prompt engineering), **applied** (real tasks, real datasets, real workflows), and **accessible** (LMIC sliding-scale pricing on paid programs).

::: {.prep-note}
Our June webinars are a good place to start — two standalone sessions on AI for research and teaching. [See the webinars →](webinars.qmd)
:::

[Webinars · June 2026]{.landing-section-title}

Two standalone 2-hour sessions. Come to one or both.

**[AI for Research](webinars.qmd#research)** (June 15, 10am–12pm ET) — How AI tools change research workflows for RAs and researchers. Live demos, Q&A.

**[AI for Teaching](webinars.qmd#teaching)** (June 17, 10am–12pm ET) — How faculty can use AI for course design and feedback.

[RA Intensive Bootcamp · Summer 2026]{.landing-section-title}

A cohort-based bootcamp for aspiring and current research assistants. Learn to use AI tools across the full RA workflow — literature review, data cleaning, code debugging, writing, and field coordination.

- **Who it's for**: Pre-docs, recent grads, RAs at research organizations (IPA, J-PAL, IDinsight, DIME, university labs)
- **Format**: Live sessions over multiple weeks, with homework and a capstone exercise
- **Pricing**: Sliding scale by country income level. Scholarships available.

[Learn more and register →](bootcamp.qmd)

[Past Events]{.landing-section-title}

**UVM Economics AI Bootcamp** (April 2026) — Two-session faculty workshop at the University of Vermont. [View materials →](uvm-bootcamp.html)

:::
